title: DocuCorp Consulting Project Plan (Aberdeen Group, ~June 2000)
author: Peter S. Kastner / Aberdeen Group
abstract: This project plan details a multi-phase Aberdeen Group management consulting engagement for DocuCorp, a document management software vendor, beginning with a two-day on-site Discovery phase in Dallas to interview managers across sales, R&D, support, and professional services, followed by a presentation of preliminary findings to DocuCorp's executive team. Subsequent phases include competitive analysis, strategic positioning recommendations, and delivery of a final report, with the overall goal of helping DocuCorp optimize its market position, organizational structure, and go-to-market strategy.
importance_rationale: A standard management consulting project plan with limited historical significance; primarily documents Aberdeen's methodology for vendor advisory engagements beyond benchmark and market-research work.
relevance_rationale: The DocuCorp-specific engagement context is historical; the document's consulting methodology framework has limited ongoing applicability.
prescience_rationale: This is a consulting project plan rather than a market analysis or predictive research document; prescience assessment is not applicable.
